30.reason: This else only attaches to the last if, so when the first or second if condition is true, the else still executes, printing the '-' character incorrectly.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  This else only attaches to the last if, so when the first or second if condition is true, the else still executes, printing the '-' character incorrectly.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  This else only attaches to the last if, not to all three. When the first or second if condition is true, the else still executes, causing the '-' to be printed incorrectly.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This else mistakenly prints the character at s[i] after expansion (which is the second endpoint) causing duplication (e.g., extra 'F' in output).
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This 'else' only pairs with the last 'if', so if any prior 'if' condition is true, this 'else' is skipped even if later conditions fail. This causes '-' to be printed unnecessarily in some cases, but more critically, due to lack of mutual exclusion among the three type checks, the logic is flawed.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This 'else' only pairs with the last 'if', not the whole group. So if the first or second 'if' matches and runs, this 'else' is still evaluated against the third 'if', possibly printing an extra '-'. This causes duplication like the extra 'F' in the output.]

18.reason: This should be "else if" to prevent fall-through after a previous condition is satisfied.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  This should be "else if" to prevent fall-through after a previous condition is satisfied.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  Should be 'else if'. Using independent 'if' statements causes the final 'else' to execute incorrectly when a previous condition is true, resulting in duplicate characters in the output.

22.reason: [After expanding, 'i' is incremented, but if multiple conditions are checked (e.g., digit and uppercase both false but lowercase true), 'i' may be incremented multiple times or incorrectly. Also, using separate 'if's instead of 'else if' causes fall-through behavior leading to duplicate output like extra 'F'.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [After expanding, 'i' is incremented, but if multiple conditions are checked (e.g., digit and uppercase both false but lowercase true), 'i' may be incremented multiple times or incorrectly. Also, using separate 'if's instead of 'else if' causes fall-through behavior leading to duplicate output like extra 'F'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [After expanding, 'i' is incremented, but if multiple conditions are checked (e.g., digit and uppercase both false, then lowercase true), 'i' may be incremented multiple times or the '-' may be printed additionally due to missing 'else if'. Also, after incrementing 'i', the next character (after '-') is skipped in the loop, but if none of the conditions match, the else clause prints '-', yet the structure allows fall-through.]

28.reason: [Same issue: multiple 'if's can cause 'i' to be incremented more than once when only one expansion should occur. This leads to skipping characters or double processing.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: using separate 'if's instead of 'else if' causes potential double expansion or incorrect control flow. Also, after incrementing 'i', the loop will skip the next character, but if this block executes, the following 'else' should not run — however, because it's a separate 'if', the final 'else' may still execute if the last condition fails, leading to duplicate output.]
